feed.title: Current Watches, Warnings and Advisories for New Zealand issued by MetService
feed.rights: Copyright Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited, 2026. Licensed under Creative Commons BY 4.0.

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 70 to 90 mm of rain. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h from evening, but possibly 25 to 40 mm/h in localised downpours. Squally thunderstorms possible. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Minimal.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, and amounts may approach warning criteria. Squally thunderstorms and localised downpours possible this evening. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 100 to 130 mm of rain. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h, especially in thunderstorms. Note, Watch upgraded to Orange Warning. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Minimal.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain with possible thunderstorms, and amounts may approach warning criteria. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate. Changes: Tararua Range has been upgraded to an Orange Warning.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain and possible thunderstorms, and amounts may approach warning criteria. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: On top of what has already fallen, expect 70 to 90 mm of rain but 120 to 150 mm about Taranaki Maunga. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h, but possibly 25 to 40 mm/h in localised downpours this afternoon and evening. Squally thunderstorms possible. Note, heavy showers are likely after the warning period ends. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Minimal.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, with thunderstorms possible. Rainfall amounts may approach warning criteria. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Severe Thunderstorm Watch
entry.summary: Updated at 1:45pm Friday - Included Great Barrier Island and extended Thunderstorm Watch period to 10pm. A front with heavy rain and embedded thunderstorms is expected to move over the northern North Island this afternoon and evening. The thunderstorms will be accompanied by heavy rain of 10-25mm/h, strong wind gust of 100km/h, and a low risk of a small tornado. For Great Barrier Island and Coromandel Peninsula, there is also a moderate risk of localised downpours of 25-40mm/h between about 5pm and 10pm today (Friday). These downpours could occur with or without thunderstorms. Rainfall of this intensity can cause surface and/or flash flooding, especially about low-lying areas such as streams, rivers or narrow valleys, and may also lead to slips. Driving conditions will also be hazardous with surface flooding and poor visibility in heavy rain.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Severe Thunderstorm Watch
entry.summary: Updated at 1:45pm Friday - Great Barrier Island moved to be included in Coromandel Peninsula Thunderstorm Watch area with later timing. A front with heavy rain and embedded thunderstorms is expected to move over the northern North Island this afternoon and evening. The thunderstorms will be accompanied by heavy rain of 10-25mm/h, strong wind gust of 100km/h, and a low risk of a small tornado. For Northland, Auckland (excluding Great Barrier Island) and Waikato, there is also a moderate risk of localised downpours of 25-40mm/h between about 3pm and 8pm today (Friday). These downpours could occur with or without thunderstorms. Rainfall of this intensity can cause surface and/or flash flooding, especially about low-lying areas such as streams, rivers or narrow valleys, and may also lead to slips. Driving conditions will also be hazardous with surface flooding and poor visibility in heavy rain.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, and amounts may approach warning criteria. Squally thunderstorms possible. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate. Changes: Timing for onset of heavy rain delayed until this afternoon.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited
